title: Once There Was Time
content:
Let no tears fall
    If then they fell not.
    If eyes told nothing,
    Now let them tell not.
    Once there was time
    For words, looks and tears:
    That time is past, is past--
    Heart, thou shalt tell not!

    Beyond any speech
    Is silence bitter,
    As between love and love
    Nothing is sweeter.
    Once there was time, time yet
    For words, looks and tears ...
    Past, past, past, past--
    Nothing so bitter!

    Now if tears come
    That then fell never;
    If eyes such sad, sad things
    Look now for ever;
    If words, looks or tears
    Tremble with telling,
    Oh, what returning voice is it whispers
    Never, never, never!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Frederick Freeman delivers a powerful performance in "Once There Was Time"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
